findings.id: cross-component-selector:src/frontend/dm/entities/entityDetailDialog.css:/* Match the overlay's own responsive padding (clamp(16px, 2.5vw, 36px) per side, see the .modal-overlay rule above) exactly, rather than guessing a fixed value — a mismatched hardcoded calc() here previously let the modal overflow the viewport. `100%` doesn't work as a substitute: this grid has no explicit track sizing, so its implicit track sizes to content instead of stretching, and percentages against an auto track collapse instead of filling it. */ width: min(1480px, calc(100vw - 2 * clamp(16px, 2.5vw, 36px))) !important; max-width: 1480px !important; height: calc(100dvh - 2 * clamp(16px, 2.5vw, 36px)) !important; max-height: calc(100dvh - 2 * clamp(16px, 2.5vw, 36px)) !important; } /* Ampliado: solo queda el eyebrow (tipo + título) — hero, tabs y footer se ocultan para que el grafo ocupe todo el espacio disponible del modal. entityDetailImageContinuation.css defines a same-tie-break-weight 20%-hero grid (`.modal-overlay.modal-overlay:has(...):not(:has(...))`) that would otherwise win the cascade on source order; these rules repeat that same doubled-class + :not() shape so ours wins on specificity instead. */ .modal-content.entity-detail-dialog:not(.entity-detail-dialog--editing).entity-detail-dialog--relations-expanded
